raw_response: {"simulated_user_message":"Honestly, I think I set those high bars because I feel like if I don't do everything perfectly, I'll fail at managing my health and lose control. But the meds and the cost feel like a constant reminder that I'm not in charge. I keep worrying that if I don't look the way I should, people will judge me or I won't be able to keep the meds. It's exhausting.","problems_referred":["perfectionism","health_anxiety","body_image_concerns","financial_stress"]}

parsed.simulated_user_message: Honestly, I think I set those high bars because I feel like if I don't do everything perfectly, I'll fail at managing my health and lose control. But the meds and the cost feel like a constant reminder that I'm not in charge. I keep worrying that if I don't look the way I should, people will judge me or I won't be able to keep the meds. It's exhausting.

parsed.final_response: It's like the pressure to be perfect is always there, looming over you, and the meds and weight gain are just adding to that feeling of losing control. It takes a lot of courage to admit when we're not in control, and I'm here to support you in exploring what that means for you, without judgment.
